description: Adaptive Quantum Cognitive Graph Network - reasoning through dynamic semantic graphs instead of transformer attention
input: The cat sits on the chair

step_1_concept_extraction.input_text: The cat sits on the chair
step_1_concept_extraction.explanation: Instead of tokens like 'The', 'cat', ' ' we extract meaningful concepts

step_2_graph_construction.explanation: Concepts are nodes, relationships are weighted edges

operations: Hadamard on all qubits (superposition = explore all concepts at once)
Hadamard on all qubits (interference = amplify strong paths)
step_3_quantum_circuit.explanation: Quantum superposition explores all concept paths simultaneously

step_4_local_simulation.explanation: The quantum circuit prefers paths with strong connections (SIT->CHAIR has highest weight 0.90)

previous_bell_state_test.explanation: Bell state ran successfully on real IBM quantum hardware

step_5_ibm_hardware.explanation: The QDCG circuit can run on real quantum computers for true quantum advantage

key_insights: Transformers predict TOKENS, humans think in CONCEPTS
Graph structure is more interpretable than attention matrices
Quantum superposition explores all paths simultaneously
No retraining needed - just update the graph
Complexity: O(E+V) vs O(n^2) for attention
research_questions: Can dynamic semantic graphs replace transformer attention for reasoning?
Which quantum algorithms best accelerate graph search and optimization?
What benchmarks compare graph-native vs token-based architectures?
How does this scale to real-world NLP tasks?
